summary_one_line: Input must declare more than 4 default attributes for one element so nbAttrs reaches maxAttrs=4 and the realloc grow-branch fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD whose ATTLIST declares >4 default-valued attributes for a single element, so xmlAddDefAttrs is re-entered enough times for the same (name,prefix) entry that defaults->nbAttrs reaches the initial maxAttrs of 4 and the true (grow/realloc) side at line 1243 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-discovered winner seeds carry a compact, repeatable DTD structure (a small <!DOCTYPE ... SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> shell) and Grimoire's structural mutators recursively splice/extend generalized token runs, letting it replicate the attribute-declaration substructure many times; the winner re-enters xmlAddDefAttrs far more often (entry-line 189 vs 60) and takes the blocker true side 30 times while the loser never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog mutates bytes (havoc + I2S substitution) but cannot synthesize a single element accumulating 5+ default-attribute declarations; its larger 368-377 byte seeds spend executions elsewhere and the blocker true side stays at T=0, so defaults->nbAttrs never reaches maxAttrs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire share the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique cannot be the differentiator; the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural/grammar-free recombination mutators, which repeat the generalized attribute-declaration substructure enough times to push nbAttrs past maxAttrs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at parser.c:1243 grows the defaults array only when nbAttrs has reached maxAttrs.
maxAttrs is initialized to 4 on first allocation, so the grow branch needs more than 4 declarations for the same element.
Taking the true side doubles maxAttrs via realloc, confirming the grow semantics of the winning branch.
The winner re-enters xmlAddDefAttrs far more often than the loser (entry-line proxy 189 vs 60).
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 30 times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1243  T=30 F=111  T=0 F=30  } else if (defaults->nbAttrs >= defaults->maxAttrs) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds are grimoire-discovered with a compact repeated DTD shell.
Loser seeds are cmplog-discovered and larger.
Both cmplog and grimoire include I2SRandReplace, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's generalization plus structural mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures to perform structural recombination.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is grimoire structural, not I2SRandReplace, so the I2S-floor lineage check is not the verification path here. Both involved fuzzers (cmplog and grimoire) run I2SRandReplace, so dash-row lineage cannot separate them; per the 10-fuzzer caveat grimoire wraps all stages in LineageMutatorWrap with an empty names list and emits nameless rows for non-I2S finds, making the dash signal non-I2S-exclusive against grimoire. The differentiator is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators, evidenced by the higher xmlAddDefAttrs re-entry count (189 vs 60) and 30 vs 0 true-side hits at the blocker.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the blocker fires after a single element accumulates >4 default attributes, yet cmplog reaches the realloc true-side as often as grimoire (i.e. grimoire's structural repetition provides no advantage over plain havoc/I2S in driving nbAttrs past maxAttrs).

weakest_evidence_point: The winning seed bytes show a compact DTD shell but do not visibly contain 5+ attribute declarations in the 64-byte dump, so the count-of-declarations claim rests on hit-count divergence rather than directly observed declaration bytes.
